verses.text: David prays for deliverance from violent men who plot evil and sharpen their tongues 'like a serpent.' Their words are poison. The malice of the wicked is often carried first by a venomous tongue.
David prays for deliverance from violent men who plot evil and sharpen their tongues 'like a serpent.' Their words are poison. The malice of the wicked is often carried first by a venomous tongue.
David prays for deliverance from violent men who plot evil and sharpen their tongues 'like a serpent.' Their words are poison. The malice of the wicked is often carried first by a venomous tongue.
He asks to be kept from the proud who hide snares and nets in his path. Traps surround him. The godly walk amid hidden dangers and need God's keeping at every step.
He asks to be kept from the proud who hide snares and nets in his path. Traps surround him. The godly walk amid hidden dangers and need God's keeping at every step.

'Thou art my God,' he declares, the 'strength of my salvation,' covering his head in battle; he asks God not to grant the wicked their desires. He claims God as his own. The one who owns God as his strength can face any plot.

'Thou art my God,' he declares, the 'strength of my salvation,' covering his head in battle; he asks God not to grant the wicked their desires. He claims God as his own. The one who owns God as his strength can face any plot.

'Thou art my God,' he declares, the 'strength of my salvation,' covering his head in battle; he asks God not to grant the wicked their desires. He claims God as his own. The one who owns God as his strength can face any plot.

He asks that the mischief of his enemies' lips fall back on their own heads and that evil hunt the violent man. Their cruelty will recoil. The wrong devised against the innocent returns upon its author.

He asks that the mischief of his enemies' lips fall back on their own heads and that evil hunt the violent man. Their cruelty will recoil. The wrong devised against the innocent returns upon its author.

He asks that the mischief of his enemies' lips fall back on their own heads and that evil hunt the violent man. Their cruelty will recoil. The wrong devised against the innocent returns upon its author.

'I know that the LORD will maintain the cause of the afflicted,' and the righteous shall dwell in His presence. Confidence ends the psalm. God takes up the case of the oppressed and brings the upright at last to dwell with Him.
